title: minuartia verna subsp. glacialis
intro: Minuartia verna subsp. glacialis, also known as glacier pusillium, is a subspecies of the minuartia verna plant, a type of flowering plant in the carnation family native to the Arctic tundra. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of glacier pusillium include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
